name: Aqaba
summary: Aqaba is Jordan's main coastal city and historically one of the safer destinations in the Middle East. However, the current US State Department Level 3 advisory due to regional tensions and terrorism risks requires increased vigilance. The city maintains strong security infrastructure and low crime rates under normal circumstances.
quickVerdict: While Aqaba normally enjoys a reputation as a safe tourist destination with excellent security measures, the current regional conflict has elevated risks significantly. The US ordered departure of non-emergency personnel in March 2026, and there are ongoing threats of drone and missile attacks from Iran. Travelers should strongly reconsider visiting at this time and have emergency evacuation plans ready. Those who do visit should maintain heightened awareness and avoid large gatherings.

neighborhoods.name: Tourist Beach Area
neighborhoods.description: Main hotel and resort strip along the Red Sea coast. Well-patrolled with good security presence from tourism police. Generally safe during day but avoid isolated areas at night.

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.description: Commercial and shopping district with restaurants and local businesses. Busy during day with reasonable foot traffic. Good for tourists but use standard precautions.

neighborhoods.name: Port Area
neighborhoods.description: Industrial port zone with limited tourist facilities. Increased security presence due to strategic importance but avoid lingering here unnecessarily.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Traditional residential area with local markets and historic sites. Generally safe but narrow streets can feel isolating. Use caution after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Airport Road
neighborhoods.description: Main highway connecting to King Hussein International Airport. Well-maintained but avoid stops in remote areas. Flash flood risk during rare rain events.

neighborhoods.name: Desert Highway Approach
neighborhoods.description: Highway connection to Amman and northern Jordan. Remote stretches with minimal services. Higher risk area due to isolation and potential security concerns.

scams.name: Fake Antique Sales
scams.description: Vendors selling supposed ancient artifacts or traditional items that are actually cheap replicas made elsewhere.
scams.howToAvoid: Only buy from reputable shops, be skeptical of any 'authentic ancient' items, and negotiate prices respectfully.

scams.name: Taxi Fare Inflation
scams.description: Drivers taking longer routes or refusing to use meters, especially targeting tourists arriving at airports or hotels.
scams.howToAvoid: Agree on fare upfront, use ride-sharing apps when available, or ask hotels to arrange trusted drivers.

scams.name: Diving Equipment Overcharging
scams.description: Dive operators inflating prices for equipment rental or certification courses, especially for inexperienced divers.
scams.howToAvoid: Research dive operators beforehand, compare prices from multiple shops, and check equipment quality before paying.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Tricks
scams.description: Money changers giving incorrect rates or short-changing customers, particularly with larger bills.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official bank exchanges, count money carefully, and avoid street money changers offering too-good-to-be-true rates.

soloFemale.overview: Aqaba is generally considered safe for solo female travelers under normal circumstances, ranking as the 4th safest place in Jordan for women. However, current regional tensions require extra caution. The city has low rates of harassment and locals are generally respectful, but cultural sensitivity regarding dress and behavior remains important.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees, even in beach areas when not at resort pools
Stay in well-reviewed hotels in tourist areas rather than isolated accommodations
Avoid walking alone after dark, especially in less populated areas
Consider joining group tours or activities rather than exploring completely solo
Keep emergency contact information readily accessible due to current security situation

nightSafety.overview: Night safety in Aqaba has been significantly impacted by current regional tensions. While the city typically has good security infrastructure, the elevated threat level means extra precautions are essential. Most tourist areas remain reasonably safe with security presence, but vigilance is crucial.
tips: Stay in tourist resort areas after dark where security presence is strongest
Avoid isolated beaches or desert areas completely at night
Use hotel-arranged transportation rather than walking or using unfamiliar taxis
Keep aware of current security alerts and be prepared to shelter in place if advised
Have multiple communication methods available and inform others of your location

transport.metro: Aqaba has no metro system. Public transport is limited to buses and service taxis.
transport.rideshare: Limited ride-sharing services available. Careem operates in some areas but coverage is inconsistent.
transport.taxis: Taxis are common but agree on fares upfront. Hotel-arranged drivers are generally more reliable and safer.
transport.tips: Private drivers or hotel transfers are safest current option given security situation. Avoid hitchhiking or unofficial transport services.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional establishments
Dress modestly especially when visiting religious sites - cover shoulders, knees, and avoid tight clothing
During Ramadan, avoid eating, drinking, or smoking in public during daylight hours
Greet with handshakes but avoid physical contact between opposite genders unless initiated
Show respect for Islamic customs and avoid public displays of affection
Accept offered hospitality (tea, food) graciously as refusal can be considered rude

health.overview: Aqaba has decent medical facilities for a city of its size, with several hospitals providing emergency care. However, serious medical situations may require evacuation to Amman. Travel insurance with medical evacuation coverage is essential.
health.water: Tap water is technically safe but has an unusual taste due to aging pipes. Bottled water is widely available and recommended for drinking.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for Jordan. Routine vaccines (MMR, DPT, flu) recommended. Consider Hepatitis A/B if eating street food.
health.altitude: Sea level location with hot, dry desert climate. Temperatures can exceed 40°C (104°F) in summer. Stay hydrated and use sun protection.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Amman: +962-6-590-6500 (emergency after hours). Note: US Embassy operations are currently limited due to March 2026 ordered departure.

faq.a: Currently, Aqaba carries elevated risks due to regional tensions. While historically very safe, the US State Department's Level 3 advisory and ordered departure of personnel in March 2026 reflect serious security concerns including potential missile and drone attacks.

Night safety is currently compromised due to regional security situation. While tourist areas maintain security presence, visitors should stay in well-secured resort areas and avoid venturing out unnecessarily after dark.

faq.q: Is Aqaba safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Under normal circumstances, Aqaba ranks among Jordan's safest cities for solo women. However, current security conditions require extra precautions. Solo female travelers should seriously reconsider visiting at this time.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Aqaba?
faq.a: Currently avoid: isolated desert areas, port industrial zones, and remote highway stretches. Stay in tourist resort areas and avoid large gatherings. Monitor security alerts closely and be prepared to shelter in place.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Aqaba?
faq.a: Public transport is limited and not recommended during current security situation. Use hotel-arranged private drivers or taxis from reputable companies. Avoid hitchhiking or unofficial transport services completely.
